zh.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen是Erwin II von Gleichen和Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen的兒子，於1170年出生在德國尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 格廷根格萊希。他於大約1200年與Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1227年9月12日在德國賀林根艾福特艾福特享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen是Erwin II von Gleichen和Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen的儿子，于1170年出生在德国尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 格廷根格萊希。他于大约1200年与Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1227年9月12日在德国賀林根艾福特艾福特享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Gleichen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational name for an iron worker who flattened out the metal into sheets, from an agent derivative of Middle High German gelīchen ‘to smooth or even out’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the inflected form of German gleich ‘same’, one of names from vocabulary words that were selected at random by government officials when surnames were made compulsory.

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lammert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lamprecht
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen was born in 1170, in Gleichen, Göttingen, Lower Saxony, Germany as the son of Erwin II von Gleichen and Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen. He married Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende about 1200. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 12 September 1227, in Erfurt, Erfurt, Thuringia, Germany, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen est né en 1170, à Gleichen, Landkreis Göttingen, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, fils d’Erwin II von Gleichen et Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen. Il a épousé Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende environ 1200. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 12 septembre 1227, à Erfurt, Erfurt, Thüringen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 57 ans.

Er hat um 1200 Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 12. September 1227 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Erfurt, Erfurt, Thüringen, Deutschland.

ko.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen(은)는 1170년 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 그o틴겐 글레이천에서 Erwin II von Gleichen(와)과 Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1200년 Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1227년 9월 12일 독일 스u링 르퍼트 르퍼트에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lambert II Erwinson von Gleichen родился в 1170 г. в местности гличэн, ландкрис гoттинген, ниедирсачсен, Германия. Его родителей звали Erwin II von Gleichen и Countess Adelheid von Orlamünde of Gleichen. Он женился на женщине по имени Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende около 1200 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 12 сентября 1227 г. в местности эрфурт, эрфурт, тuринген, Германия в возрасте 57 лет.

Se casó con Gräfin Sofie von Orlamuende aproximadamente 1200. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 12 de septiembre de 1227, en Erfurt, Erfurt, Thüringen, Alemania, a la edad de 57 años.
